data.name: Msg_ConfirmDelete
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete this item?

data.name: Parking_NoLots
data.value: No parking lots configured. Create one to define slot ranges and timeouts.

data.name: Ext_DeleteConfirm
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete extension

data.name: Trunk_DeleteConfirm
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete trunk

data.name: Trunk_TestCallSuccess
data.value: Test call originated successfully through trunk '{0}'.

data.name: Trunk_TestCallNoServer
data.value: Server not available for test call.

data.name: Routes_DeleteConfirm
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete route

data.name: RouteEdit_NewInbound
data.value: New Inbound Route — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: RouteEdit_NewOutbound
data.value: New Outbound Route — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: RouteEdit_EditInbound
data.value: Edit Inbound Route — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: RouteEdit_EditOutbound
data.value: Edit Outbound Route — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: RouteEdit_NoTrunks
data.value: No trunks added. At least one trunk is required.

data.name: Ivr_NoMenus
data.value: No IVR menus configured. Click "New IVR" to create one.

data.name: IvrEdit_NameHint
data.value: Alphanumeric + hyphens. Used in dialplan context name.

data.name: IvrEdit_GreetingHint
data.value: Audio filename without extension, as Asterisk expects.

data.name: TC_DeleteConfirm
data.value: Are you sure you want to delete

data.name: TCEdit_NewTitle
data.value: New Time Condition — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: TCEdit_EditTitle
data.value: Edit Time Condition — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: TCEdit_NameHint
data.value: Will be used as dialplan context name. Alphanumeric and hyphens only.

data.name: QDetail_DeleteConfirm
data.value: and all its members? This cannot be undone.

data.name: QDetail_NotConfigured
data.value: Not configured in database. Click "Configure" to set up this queue.

data.name: HelpPanel_QueueDetail_Members
data.value: Queue members: shows each member's device status, calls taken, penalty, and pause state. You can pause/unpause members or remove them from the queue.

data.name: Rec_NoPolicies
data.value: No recording policies. Click "+ New Policy" to create one.

data.name: Rec_FileAccessUnavail
data.value: Recording file access unavailable. Check RecordingsPath config for this server.

data.name: RecEdit_NewTitle
data.value: New Recording Policy — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: RecEdit_EditTitle
data.value: Edit Recording Policy — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: Moh_Title
data.value: Music on Hold — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: Moh_NoClasses
data.value: No MOH classes configured. Click "+ New Class" to create one.

data.name: MohEdit_NewTitle
data.value: New MOH Class — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: MohEdit_EditTitle
data.value: Edit MOH Class — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: MohEdit_Mp3Deprecated
data.value: Mode "mp3" is deprecated since Asterisk 14+. Consider using "files" with the format_mp3 module.

data.name: MohEdit_FileAccessUnavail
data.value: File access unavailable for this MOH directory.

data.name: Conf_NoProfiles
data.value: No conference profiles configured. Create one to define ConfBridge bridge settings.

data.name: ConfEdit_NewTitle
data.value: New Conference Profile — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: ConfEdit_EditTitle
data.value: Edit Conference Profile — Asterisk PBX Admin

data.name: ConfEdit_NumberHint
data.value: Dial-in extension for this conference room

data.name: Login_Prompt
data.value: Authenticate to access the monitoring console

data.name: SS_Choose
data.value: Choose an Asterisk server to monitor

data.name: Help_ExtNumber
data.value: This is the internal phone number. Employees dial this to reach each other (e.g., 2001).

data.name: Help_DisplayName
data.value: The name shown on caller ID when this person makes a call. Usually the employee's full name.

data.name: Help_Technology
data.value: The protocol your phone uses to connect. PJSIP is the modern standard — use it unless you have old phones that require SIP or IAX2.

data.name: Help_Password
data.value: The secret the phone uses to register with the PBX. Use a strong, random password (the Generate button helps). Minimum 8 characters.

data.name: Help_Context
data.value: Controls which calls this phone can make. 'default' means it can call other extensions and outside lines. Think of it as a permission group.

data.name: Help_CallGroup
data.value: Group number for call pickup. Phones in the same group can pick up each other's ringing calls. Usually '1' for the main office.

data.name: Help_PickupGroup
data.value: Which call groups this phone can pick up. If set to '1', this phone can answer ringing calls from group 1 by dialing the pickup code.

data.name: Help_Codecs
data.value: Audio quality settings. Think of it like choosing between MP3 (small, decent quality) and CD (big, perfect quality). ulaw and alaw are the safe defaults.

data.name: Help_DtmfMode
data.value: How the phone sends button-press tones (the beeps when you press 1, 2, 3...). Leave as 'RFC 4733' unless your phone provider says otherwise.

data.name: Help_Transport
data.value: How data travels between the phone and PBX. UDP is fastest (default). TCP is more reliable. TLS adds encryption for security.

data.name: Help_TrunkName
data.value: A friendly name to identify this connection. Example: 'Main-Provider' or 'Backup-Line'. Only for your reference.

data.name: Help_TrunkHost
data.value: The address of your phone company's server. Your provider gives you this (e.g., sip.provider.com or an IP address).

data.name: Help_TrunkPort
data.value: Network port for the connection. Default is 5060 for SIP and 5061 for TLS. Only change if your provider specifies a different one.

data.name: Help_TrunkTransport
data.value: How data travels to your provider. UDP is standard. Use TLS if your provider requires encrypted connections.

data.name: Help_TrunkUsername
data.value: The account name your phone provider gave you for authentication. Sometimes called 'Auth ID' or 'SIP Username'.

data.name: Help_TrunkSecret
data.value: The password for your trunk account. Your phone provider gives you this. Keep it secure — it controls access to your phone lines.

data.name: Help_TrunkAuthType
data.value: How the PBX proves its identity to the provider. 'MD5' is the standard method. Only change if your provider requires something different.

data.name: Help_TrunkCodecs
data.value: Audio formats supported by this trunk. Must match what your provider supports. ulaw (North America) and alaw (Europe) are universal standards.

data.name: Help_QueueName
data.value: A unique name for this call queue. Example: 'sales' or 'support'. Callers are placed here when they call and wait for an available agent.

data.name: Help_QueueStrategy
data.value: How incoming calls are distributed. 'Ring All' rings everyone at once. 'Least Recent' rings the person who's been idle longest. 'Round Robin' takes turns.

data.name: Help_QueueMaxCallers
data.value: Maximum number of callers that can wait in the queue at once. When full, new callers hear a busy signal or get redirected.

data.name: Help_QueueTimeout
data.value: How many seconds to ring an agent before trying the next one (or trying again). 15-30 seconds is typical.

data.name: Help_QueueMembers
data.value: The extensions (agents) assigned to answer calls in this queue. Add extensions by number. They'll receive calls based on the strategy above.

data.name: Help_QueueServiceLevel
data.value: Your target answer time in seconds. If you set 60, the system tracks what percentage of calls are answered within 1 minute. Used for reporting.

data.name: Help_RouteName
data.value: A descriptive name for this route. Example: 'Main-Number-Inbound' or 'International-Calls'. Only for your reference.

data.name: Help_RouteDIDPattern
data.value: The phone number that callers dial to reach you from outside. Your phone company assigns this. Use '_X.' for wildcard patterns.

data.name: Help_RoutePriority
data.value: When multiple routes match, the one with the lowest priority number wins. Use 1 for your main route, 2 for backup, etc.

data.name: Help_RouteDestType
data.value: Where to send matched calls. Extension = a specific phone. Queue = a group of agents. IVR = an automated menu ('Press 1 for...').

data.name: Help_RouteTarget
data.value: The specific extension number, queue name, or IVR menu that will receive the call.

data.name: Help_RouteTrunk
data.value: Which phone line to use for outbound calls matching this route. Order matters — if the first trunk is busy, the system tries the next one.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtEdit_Identity
data.value: This is where you set up a new phone line for someone in your office. Enter their name and assign an extension number — think of it like giving them their own desk phone number.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtEdit_Auth
data.value: Security settings for this extension. The password is what the physical phone uses to connect to the system — like a Wi-Fi password but for the phone. Always use a strong, random password.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtEdit_Dialplan
data.value: Controls what this phone is allowed to do. The context sets permissions (who can they call?), and groups determine which colleagues can pick up their ringing phone.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtEdit_MediaTransport
data.value: Technical settings for audio quality and network connection. The defaults work for most setups. Only change these if you're experiencing audio issues or your IT team recommends specific settings.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtEdit_Voicemail
data.value: Set up a voicemail box so callers can leave messages when this person is unavailable. The PIN protects the voicemail from unauthorized access. Email notifications send new messages to the user's inbox.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtEdit_CallFeatures
data.value: Call forwarding rules. Forward calls when the user is busy, doesn't answer, or always. DND (Do Not Disturb) sends all calls straight to voicemail.

data.name: HelpPanel_TrunkEdit_Connection
data.value: Connection details for your phone provider. Think of a trunk as the phone line connecting your office to the outside world. Your provider gives you the host address and port.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtEdit_Auth_Trunk
data.value: Credentials your phone provider gave you. The PBX uses these to authenticate with the provider, like logging into your phone company's service.

data.name: HelpPanel_QCfg_Identity
data.value: Basic information about this call queue. A queue holds incoming callers in line and distributes them to available agents — like a waiting room with an automated receptionist.

data.name: HelpPanel_QCfg_StrategyTiming
data.value: How calls are distributed to agents. The strategy decides who rings first, and the timeout controls how long to wait before trying the next agent.

data.name: HelpPanel_RouteEdit_RouteDetails
data.value: Defines which calls this route handles. For inbound routes, the DID pattern matches the number callers dial. For outbound routes, the dial pattern matches what your employees dial.

data.name: HelpPanel_QCfg_StaticMembers
data.value: The agents assigned to answer calls in this queue. Add them by their device address (e.g., PJSIP/2001). Penalty controls priority — lower penalty agents get calls first.

data.name: HelpPanel_RouteEdit_TrunksOrder
data.value: The phone lines used for this outbound route, in priority order. If the first trunk is busy or down, the system automatically tries the next one in the list.

data.name: Glossary_Extension_Def
data.value: An internal phone number assigned to a person or device. When someone dials 2001, the phone registered to extension 2001 rings.

data.name: Glossary_Extension_Tip
data.value: Each employee typically gets one extension. Number ranges are set in system settings.

data.name: Glossary_Context_Def
data.value: A permission group that controls what calls a phone can make. 'default' allows internal and external calls. Think of contexts as security zones.

data.name: Glossary_Context_Tip
data.value: Most extensions use 'default'. Lobby phones might use a restricted context.

data.name: Glossary_Codec_Def
data.value: An audio compression format. Like choosing between MP3 and WAV — smaller files vs. better quality. Codecs determine call audio quality and bandwidth usage.

data.name: Glossary_Codec_Tip
data.value: ulaw (North America) and alaw (Europe) are universal. G.722 gives HD audio if both sides support it.

data.name: Glossary_DTMF_Def
data.value: The tones your phone sends when you press buttons (the beeps for 1, 2, 3...). Used for phone menus ('Press 1 for sales') and entering PINs.

data.name: Glossary_DTMF_Tip
data.value: RFC 4733 is the modern standard. Only change if IVR menus aren't responding to button presses.

data.name: Glossary_Trunk_Def
data.value: A connection between your phone system and the outside world. Like a phone line that lets your employees make and receive external calls. You get trunks from a phone provider.

data.name: Glossary_Trunk_Tip
data.value: Most offices need at least one trunk. Add a second for redundancy in case the first provider has issues.

data.name: Glossary_DID_Def
data.value: Direct Inward Dialing — the external phone number that people call to reach your office. Your phone provider assigns DIDs, and you map them to extensions, queues, or IVRs.

data.name: Glossary_DID_Tip
data.value: You can have many DIDs pointing to different departments (sales, support) on the same trunk.

data.name: Glossary_Route_Def
data.value: A rule that tells the PBX where to send a call. Inbound routes match incoming DID numbers. Outbound routes match dialed patterns and pick which trunk to use.

data.name: Glossary_Route_Tip
data.value: Lower priority number = higher importance. Route priority decides which rule wins when multiple routes match.

data.name: Glossary_Queue_Def
data.value: A virtual waiting room for callers. When someone calls, they hear hold music while the system finds an available agent to answer. Queues distribute calls fairly among team members.

data.name: Glossary_Queue_Tip
data.value: 'Ring All' is simplest — everyone's phone rings. 'Least Recent' is fairest — idle agents get calls first.

data.name: Glossary_Agent_Def
data.value: A person who answers calls in a queue. Agents log in and out of queues, and can pause to take a break. The system tracks their availability and call statistics.

data.name: Glossary_Agent_Tip
data.value: Agents can belong to multiple queues. Their status (Available, Paused, In Call) shows on the monitoring dashboard.

data.name: Glossary_IVR_Def
data.value: Interactive Voice Response — the automated menu callers hear ('Press 1 for sales, 2 for support'). IVRs route calls based on what buttons the caller presses.

data.name: Glossary_IVR_Tip
data.value: Keep menus short (3-4 options max). Always offer 'Press 0 for operator' as an escape route.

data.name: Glossary_SIP_Def
data.value: Session Initiation Protocol — the standard language phones use to talk to the PBX. It handles call setup, ringing, answering, and hanging up. Most modern phones speak SIP.

data.name: Glossary_SIP_Tip
data.value: SIP is the older implementation in Asterisk. For new setups, use PJSIP instead.

data.name: Glossary_PJSIP_Def
data.value: The modern SIP implementation in Asterisk. Does the same thing as SIP but with better performance, more features, and active development. The recommended choice for new extensions.

data.name: Glossary_PJSIP_Tip
data.value: Always choose PJSIP for new extensions unless you have legacy phones that specifically need chan_sip.

data.name: Glossary_Voicemail_Def
data.value: A virtual answering machine. When someone doesn't answer their phone, callers can leave a voice message. Messages can be emailed as audio attachments.

data.name: Glossary_Voicemail_Tip
data.value: Set a PIN to prevent unauthorized access. Enable email notifications so users get messages in their inbox.

data.name: Glossary_MOH_Def
data.value: Music on Hold — what callers hear while waiting in a queue or on hold. Can be music files, promotional messages, or a mix. Different queues can have different hold music.

data.name: Glossary_MOH_Tip
data.value: Use royalty-free music. Avoid silence — callers will think they've been disconnected.

data.name: Glossary_Park_Def
data.value: Putting a call in a 'parking lot' where anyone can pick it up by dialing the parking slot number. Useful when transferring calls in an office — 'Call for John on parking 701'.

data.name: Glossary_Park_Tip
data.value: Parked calls have a timeout. If nobody picks up in time, the call rings back to the person who parked it.

data.name: Err_ExtOutOfRange
data.value: Extension must be within the configured range.

data.name: Err_PasswordTooShort
data.value: Password must be at least 8 characters.

data.name: Err_InvalidHost
data.value: Enter a valid hostname or IP address.

data.name: Err_VoicemailPinRequired
data.value: PIN is required when voicemail is enabled.

data.name: Err_CFNoAnswerTimeout
data.value: Timeout is required when call forward no-answer is set.

data.name: Err_NoTrunks
data.value: At least one trunk is required for outbound routes.

data.name: Err_DIDRequired
data.value: DID pattern is required for inbound routes.

data.name: Template_StandardEmployee
data.value: Standard Employee — Basic phone with voicemail

data.name: Template_LobbyPhone
data.value: Lobby Phone — Shared phone, no voicemail

data.name: Template_Manager
data.value: Manager — Full features with HD audio

data.name: Help_ConfName
data.value: The name that identifies this conference room in the system.

data.name: Help_ConfNumber
data.value: The extension number users dial to join this conference (e.g., 6000).

data.name: Help_ConfMaxMembers
data.value: Maximum number of participants allowed at the same time. 0 means unlimited.

data.name: Help_ConfUserPin
data.value: Optional PIN that regular participants must enter to join. Leave empty for open access.

data.name: Help_ConfAdminPin
data.value: Optional PIN for the conference administrator. The admin can mute/unmute and kick participants.

data.name: Help_ConfAutoRecord
data.value: When enabled, the conference is automatically recorded from the moment the first participant joins.

data.name: Help_ConfMohClass
data.value: The music that plays when only one person is in the conference waiting for others to join.

data.name: HelpPanel_ConfEdit_BridgeSettings
data.value: Set up the conference bridge: give it a name, assign an extension number that people dial to join, and set how many participants can be in the room at once.

data.name: HelpPanel_ConfEdit_Security
data.value: Security PINs to control who can join the conference. The user PIN is for regular participants, and the admin PIN gives extra controls like muting and kicking. Leave empty for open access.

data.name: HelpPanel_ConfEdit_Options
data.value: Additional options: enable auto-recording to capture the conference audio, and choose what music plays while waiting for others to join.

data.name: Help_MohName
data.value: Name for this music class. Use descriptive names like 'jazz' or 'classical'.

data.name: Help_MohMode
data.value: How audio is played: 'files' reads from a folder, 'custom' runs an external command. Avoid 'mp3' — it's deprecated.

data.name: Help_MohDirectory
data.value: Server folder containing the audio files. Asterisk reads all supported files from this directory.

data.name: Help_MohSort
data.value: Play files in random order (shuffle) or alphabetically. Random is more natural for callers on hold.

data.name: Help_MohCustomApp
data.value: External command to generate audio. For example, an mp3 streaming command. Must output 8kHz mono audio.

data.name: HelpPanel_MohEdit_ClassSettings
data.value: Set up the music class: give it a name and choose how audio is played — from local files on the server or via an external streaming command.

data.name: HelpPanel_MohEdit_AudioFiles
data.value: Upload and manage audio files for this class. Supported formats include WAV, GSM, ulaw, alaw, and more. Files play to callers waiting on hold or in queue.

data.name: Help_RecName
data.value: Descriptive name for this recording policy (e.g., 'Sales Calls', 'Support Queue').

data.name: Help_RecMode
data.value: Which calls to record: all calls, only incoming, only outgoing, or on-demand.

data.name: Help_RecFormat
data.value: Audio format: WAV (best quality, larger files), WAV49 (compressed WAV), GSM (smallest, lower quality).

data.name: Help_RecStoragePath
data.value: Server folder where recordings are saved. Default: /var/spool/asterisk/monitor/

data.name: Help_RecRetentionDays
data.value: Days to keep recordings before automatic deletion. 0 means keep forever.

data.name: Help_RecMixMonitorOpts
data.value: Advanced MixMonitor flags: 'r' reads from caller side, 't' reads from agent side.

data.name: HelpPanel_RecEdit_PolicySettings
data.value: Define which calls to record, in what format, and how long to keep them. Think of this as the 'rules' for recording — like saying 'record all sales calls in WAV format and keep them for 90 days'.

data.name: HelpPanel_RecEdit_Targets
data.value: Select specific extensions, queues, or trunks to apply this recording policy to. Only calls involving these targets will be recorded.

data.name: Help_IvrName
data.value: Internal name for this IVR menu. Used in routing rules and logs.

data.name: Help_IvrLabel
data.value: Friendly display name shown in the admin panel.

data.name: Help_IvrEnabled
data.value: When enabled, this IVR menu is active and can receive calls. Disable to take it offline temporarily.

data.name: Help_IvrGreeting
data.value: Sound file that plays when a caller enters this menu (e.g., 'welcome-message'). Must exist on the Asterisk server.

data.name: Help_IvrTimeout
data.value: Seconds to wait for the caller to press a key before triggering the timeout destination.

data.name: Help_IvrMaxRetries
data.value: How many times the caller can press an invalid key before being sent to the invalid destination.

data.name: Help_IvrTimeoutDest
data.value: Where to send the caller if they don't press any key within the timeout period.

data.name: Help_IvrInvalidDest
data.value: Where to send the caller after exhausting all retries with invalid key presses.

data.name: HelpPanel_IvrEdit_Identity
data.value: Basic IVR menu identification: name, label, and whether it's active. The name is used internally for routing, while the label is what you see in this admin panel.

data.name: HelpPanel_IvrEdit_Audio
data.value: The greeting audio file that plays when a caller enters this menu — for example, 'Press 1 for sales, press 2 for support'. The file must already exist on the Asterisk server.

data.name: HelpPanel_IvrEdit_Behavior
data.value: How long to wait for the caller to press a key, and how many chances they get before being redirected. These prevent callers from getting stuck in silence.

data.name: HelpPanel_IvrEdit_Options
data.value: DTMF options: each key (0-9, *, #) can point to a different destination — an extension, queue, submenu, voicemail, or hangup. This is the menu tree the caller navigates.

data.name: HelpPanel_IvrEdit_Fallbacks
data.value: What happens when the caller doesn't press anything (timeout) or presses an invalid key. Always set these so callers don't get stuck.

data.name: Help_TcName
data.value: Internal name for this time condition (e.g., 'business-hours', 'holiday-schedule').

data.name: Help_TcEnabled
data.value: When enabled, this condition actively routes calls. Disable to bypass it.

data.name: Help_TcMatchType
data.value: Destination type when the current time matches the schedule (extension, queue, or another time condition).

data.name: Help_TcMatchTarget
data.value: The specific destination (extension number, queue name) for calls during business hours.

data.name: Help_TcNoMatchType
data.value: Destination type when the current time does NOT match (after hours, holidays).

data.name: Help_TcNoMatchTarget
data.value: The specific destination for non-matching calls (e.g., voicemail, after-hours queue).

data.name: HelpPanel_TcEdit_Identity
data.value: Basic identification: give the time condition a name and choose whether it's active. Think of it as a traffic light for calls — it decides which way they go based on the clock.

data.name: HelpPanel_TcEdit_Schedule
data.value: Weekly schedule: enable/disable each day and set business hours. Calls during these hours go to the match destination, and calls outside go to the no-match destination.

data.name: HelpPanel_TcEdit_Holidays
data.value: Special dates when the normal schedule is overridden — calls go to the no-match destination regardless of the time. Great for holidays, company events, or maintenance windows.

data.name: HelpPanel_TcEdit_Destinations
data.value: Where to send calls: the match destination handles calls during business hours (e.g., ring the front desk), and the no-match destination handles after hours (e.g., go to voicemail).

data.name: HelpPanel_Home_KPIs
data.value: Real-time key indicators: active calls, queues, available/on-call/paused agents. Numbers refresh every 2 seconds — like a car dashboard for your phone system.

data.name: HelpPanel_Home_CallFlowMetrics
data.value: Call flow metrics from the Session Engine: total calls tracked, average wait time in queue, average talk time, and abandon rate. These help you spot trends — a rising abandon rate may mean you need more agents.

data.name: HelpPanel_Home_QueueSummary
data.value: Summary of all queues: member count, waiting callers, completed/abandoned calls, and average hold/talk times. Click a queue name for details.

data.name: HelpPanel_Calls_Overview
data.value: Real-time call monitor with three views: Kanban groups calls by state, Table shows everything in a list, and Matrix lets you control calls (hold, transfer, hangup).

data.name: HelpPanel_Agents_Overview
data.value: Agent cards color-coded by state: green = available, red = on call, yellow = paused, gray = logged off. Each card shows calls taken, average times, and assigned queue.

data.name: HelpPanel_Channels_Overview
data.value: Low-level technical view: all active Asterisk channels with state, caller ID, and duration. Think of channels as the 'pipes' that carry calls.

data.name: HelpPanel_Channels_Originate
data.value: Create a call manually: specify the source channel (e.g., PJSIP/2000), context, and destination extension. Useful for testing and administrative calls.

data.name: HelpPanel_Queues_Overview
data.value: Queues distribute calls among agents based on a strategy. Cards are color-coded: gray = no activity, green = agents ready, yellow = few callers waiting, red = many waiting. The gear icon opens configuration.

data.name: HelpPanel_Extensions_Overview
data.value: Manage extensions (internal phone lines). Filter by technology (PJSIP, SIP, IAX2) or registration status. Green = connected and ready, red = disconnected. Icons show voicemail, DND, or active forwarding.

data.name: HelpPanel_Trunks_Overview
data.value: Trunks connect your PBX to the outside world (SIP providers, phone lines). Green = registered and working, red = unreachable. Shows active calls and max channels per trunk.

data.name: HelpPanel_Routes_Inbound
data.value: Inbound routes: define what happens when someone calls your numbers (DID). Each route maps a number pattern to a destination: extension, queue, or time condition. Priority determines evaluation order.

data.name: HelpPanel_Routes_Outbound
data.value: Outbound routes: define which trunk carries calls based on the dialed number. Dial patterns filter which numbers apply, and multiple trunks can be assigned as fallback.

data.name: HelpPanel_TimeConditions_Overview
data.value: Time conditions: route calls differently based on schedule (e.g., business hours vs after hours). Each condition has day/time ranges and two destinations: match and no-match. Can be manually forced open or closed.

data.name: HelpPanel_IvrMenus_Overview
data.value: IVR menus (auto-attendant): play a greeting and wait for the caller to press a key to direct their call. Each option (1, 2, 3...) points to a different destination. Submenus create navigation trees.

data.name: HelpPanel_Conferences_Live
data.value: Active conference rooms in real-time. Shows participants, who is talking, muted, or is admin. You can lock the room, mute participants, kick users, or start/stop recording.

data.name: HelpPanel_Conferences_Config
data.value: Conference profiles: reusable templates that define max capacity, security PINs, auto-recording, and hold music. Each conference room uses one of these profiles.

data.name: HelpPanel_FeatureCodes_Overview
data.value: Feature codes (star codes): shortcuts users dial to activate functions. For example, *72 for call forwarding or *60 for do not disturb. You can create, edit, and enable/disable each code.

data.name: Help_FcCode
data.value: The code users dial on their phone (e.g., *72, #21). Usually starts with * or #.

data.name: Help_FcName
data.value: Descriptive name for the code, like 'Call Forward' or 'Do Not Disturb'.

data.name: Help_FcDescription
data.value: Explanation of what this code does when dialed.

data.name: Help_FcEnabled
data.value: When active, users can dial this code. Disabling it turns it off without deleting it.

data.name: HelpPanel_MusicOnHold_Overview
data.value: Music on hold classes: groups of audio files that play when a caller is on hold or in queue. Each class can use local files or external streaming. The 'default' class is used when no other is specified.

data.name: HelpPanel_Parking_Live
data.value: Parked calls: calls 'parked' in a numbered slot so another user can pick them up by dialing that number. Shows channel, caller ID, and time remaining before the call returns to the parker.

data.name: HelpPanel_Parking_LotConfig
data.value: Parking lots: define slot ranges (e.g., 701-720), maximum wait time, and context. You can create multiple lots for different departments.

data.name: Help_ParkLotName
data.value: Identifier name for the parking lot (e.g., 'default', 'sales').

data.name: Help_ParkStartSlot
data.value: First number in the slot range (e.g., 701). Users dial this number to pick up a parked call.

data.name: Help_ParkEndSlot
data.value: Last number in the range (e.g., 720). More slots = more calls can be parked at once.

data.name: Help_ParkTimeout
data.value: Seconds a call can stay parked before automatically returning to the person who parked it.

data.name: Help_ParkContext
data.value: Asterisk context where parking is executed. Usually 'parkedcalls'.

data.name: Help_ParkMoh
data.value: Music on hold class that plays to the parked caller while they wait to be picked up.

data.name: HelpPanel_Voicemail_Overview
data.value: Voicemail mailboxes: shows all configured mailboxes with new and old message counts. Expand a mailbox to see individual messages with date, duration, and caller. You can delete messages from here.

data.name: HelpPanel_Recordings_Policies
data.value: Recording policies: rules that define which calls get recorded, in what format, and how long they're kept. Each policy can target specific extensions, queues, or trunks.

data.name: HelpPanel_Recordings_Files
data.value: Recording files stored on the server. You can play them directly or download them. Files exceeding policy retention are marked with a warning.

data.name: HelpPanel_Recordings_Active
data.value: Recordings in progress: calls being recorded right now. You can stop an active recording if needed.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtDetail_Status
data.value: Extension registration status: shows if the phone is connected, from which IP, what software it uses (User Agent), and connection latency. Green = registered, red = unreachable.

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtDetail_Features
data.value: Extension features you can toggle live: Do Not Disturb (DND) rejects all calls, and forwards redirect calls to another number (unconditional, busy, or no answer).

data.name: HelpPanel_ExtDetail_RawConfig
data.value: Complete technical configuration as it appears in Asterisk config files (pjsip.conf, sip.conf). Useful for verifying advanced parameters or diagnosing issues.

data.name: HelpPanel_TrunkDetail_Status
data.value: Trunk status: shows if it's registered with the provider, host and port, transport, negotiated codecs, and active calls. Latency indicates connection health.

data.name: HelpPanel_TrunkDetail_RawConfig
data.value: Complete technical trunk configuration in Asterisk files. For PJSIP shows separate sections: endpoint, authentication, AOR, and registration.

data.name: HelpPanel_CallDetail_Summary
data.value: Call summary: who is calling, where to, which queue it passed through, which agent answered. Shows technical identifiers (channels) and exact start/end times.

data.name: HelpPanel_CallDetail_Timeline
data.value: Gantt-style visual timeline: each participant has a bar showing the states it went through (dialing, ringing, connected, on hold). See the complete call sequence at a glance.

data.name: HelpPanel_CallDetail_Events
data.value: Chronological log of all AMI events that occurred during the call. Each event shows relative time, type, involved channels, and details. Like a technical log for diagnosis.

data.name: HelpPanel_QueueDetail_Stats
data.value: Real-time queue statistics: current calls, hold time, talk time, completed and abandoned counts. The strategy determines how calls are distributed among agents.

data.name: HelpPanel_QueueDetail_Agents
data.value: Queue agents (members): shows each agent's status, calls taken, penalty, and pause state. You can pause/unpause agents or remove them from the queue.

data.name: HelpPanel_QueueDetail_Callers
data.value: Callers currently waiting in queue: position, caller ID, channel, and wait time. Higher positions get answered first.

data.name: HelpPanel_Metrics_SystemInfo
data.value: Asterisk server info: installed version, uptime, active and max calls. This data loads once when the page opens.

data.name: HelpPanel_Metrics_AmiHealth
data.value: AMI connection health (management protocol): events received, dropped, and dispatched, actions sent and responses received. Dropped events or reconnections may indicate overload.

data.name: HelpPanel_Metrics_LiveGauges
data.value: Live SDK gauges: active channels, queues, and agent states (available, on call, paused). Updated every 2 seconds.

data.name: HelpPanel_Metrics_Sessions
data.value: Session engine metrics: calls created, completed, failed, timed out, and orphaned. Histograms show averages for wait, talk, hold, and total duration.

data.name: HelpPanel_Events_Overview
data.value: Real-time AMI event stream: the last 50 events from the server. Filter by type (Channel, Queue, Agent) to focus on what interests you. Useful for live monitoring and diagnosis.

data.name: HelpPanel_Console_Overview
data.value: Asterisk command console: run CLI commands remotely via AMI, as if you were at the server terminal. Quick buttons run the most common commands. History keeps the last 20 commands with results.

data.name: HelpPanel_Traffic_Overview
data.value: Phone traffic statistics: call volume, answer rate, average duration, and wait times. Enable advanced mode to see breakdown by state, top active queues, and agents with most calls handled.

data.name: Glossary_ConferenceBridge_Def
data.value: Virtual room where multiple people can talk at the same time. Asterisk supports ConfBridge (modern) and MeetMe (legacy).

data.name: Glossary_ConferenceBridge_Tip
data.value: Use ConfBridge for new setups — it's more flexible and actively maintained.

data.name: Glossary_PIN_Def
data.value: Numeric code to control access to conferences or voicemail boxes. Like a meeting access code.

data.name: Glossary_PIN_Tip
data.value: Always set an admin PIN on public conferences to prevent abuse.

data.name: Glossary_Recording_Def
data.value: Audio capture of a call to a file. MixMonitor records both sides of the conversation into a single file.

data.name: Glossary_Recording_Tip
data.value: Check local laws — many jurisdictions require consent before recording calls.

data.name: Glossary_CDR_Def
data.value: Call Detail Record: automatic log of each call with origin, destination, duration, and result. Like a call receipt.

data.name: Glossary_CDR_Tip
data.value: CDRs are stored in the database and used for billing and reporting.

data.name: Glossary_FeatureCode_Def
data.value: Short code (e.g., *72) that users dial to activate features like forwarding, DND, or call pickup.

data.name: Glossary_FeatureCode_Tip
data.value: Print a quick-reference card of active feature codes for your users.

data.name: Glossary_TimeCondition_Def
data.value: Rule that directs calls to different destinations based on day and time. Typical: business hours vs after hours.

data.name: Glossary_TimeCondition_Tip
data.value: Create separate conditions for holidays and override them manually when needed.

data.name: Glossary_CallParking_Def
data.value: 'Park' a call in a numbered slot so another person can pick it up by dialing that number.

data.name: Glossary_CallParking_Tip
data.value: Useful in offices where someone needs to transfer a call to a person whose extension they don't know.

data.name: Glossary_Originate_Def
data.value: Create a call from the system: Asterisk calls the specified channel and connects it to the destination extension.

data.name: Glossary_Originate_Tip
data.value: Used by click-to-call features and admin console testing.

data.name: Glossary_Channel_Def
data.value: The 'pipe' that carries a call. Each participant has their own channel (e.g., PJSIP/2000-00000001).

data.name: Glossary_Channel_Tip
data.value: A bridged call has two channels connected together — one for each side.

data.name: Glossary_MixMonitor_Def
data.value: Asterisk command that records both sides of an active call without interrupting it.

data.name: Glossary_MixMonitor_Tip
data.value: Replaces the older Monitor command. Records both directions (rx+tx) into one file.

data.name: Glossary_Dialplan_Def
data.value: Set of rules Asterisk follows to process each call: what to do when someone dials a number.

data.name: Glossary_Dialplan_Tip
data.value: The dialplan lives in extensions.conf (or the database in Realtime mode).

data.name: Glossary_AOR_Def
data.value: Address of Record: PJSIP registration that associates an extension with the IP address of the connected phone.

data.name: Glossary_AOR_Tip
data.value: Each PJSIP endpoint has an AOR that tracks where the phone is registered from.

data.name: DP_NoContexts
data.value: No contexts found. Refresh to load from Asterisk.

data.name: DP_DeleteContextConfirm
data.value: Delete context '{0}'? All extensions will be removed.

data.name: DP_SelectContext
data.value: Select a context from the list to view details.

data.name: DP_IncludeNotSupported
data.value: Include management is not supported in Realtime mode.

data.name: CF_NoWarnings
data.value: All systems healthy. No issues detected.

data.name: CF_SelectDid
data.value: Select an inbound route to view its call flow.

data.name: CF_TraceNoRoute
data.value: No route found for this number.
